core.database.manager_oldversion module

DATABASE MANAGER – Module 4: Persistent Storage for A‑LEMS Experiments

PURPOSE:

This module provides a complete SQLite database interface for storing all experiment data generated by the A‑LEMS system. It implements the final 10‑table schema designed for research‑grade analysis, including runs, orchestration events, high‑frequency samples, tax summaries, and a flattened ML view.

WHY IT EXISTS:

  • To enable long‑term storage and reproducibility of all measurements.

  • To support GUI queries (fast, indexed) for interactive exploration.

  • To provide a single flat table (ml_features) for machine learning training.

  • To preserve all raw and derived data for future analysis without having to re‑run experiments.

UNITS AND SCIENTIFIC ASSUMPTIONS:

  • All times are stored in nanoseconds (ns) for integer precision.

  • All energies are stored in microjoules (µJ) to avoid floating‑point drift.

  • Power is stored in watts (J/s) as REAL.

  • Frequencies in MHz (megahertz), temperatures in °C.

  • Baseline correction: dynamic_energy = total_energy - idle_baseline_energy.

  • Orchestration tax = agentic_dynamic - linear_dynamic (paired runs).

  • Tax is distributed proportionally to each event’s energy.

  • All derived columns (energy_per_instruction, etc.) are computed in Python to ensure correctness and are stored for ML convenience.

  • We assume that linear and agentic runs are paired by run_number within the same experiment.

WHAT IT MUST NEVER BREAK:

  • Must never lose data: all inserts are transactional.

  • Must never corrupt foreign key relationships.

  • Must never insert duplicate runs (enforced by unique index).

  • Must never miscalculate tax due to missing pairs.

AUTHOR: Deepak Panigrahy

class core.database.manager_oldversion.DatabaseManager(db_path: str = 'data/experiments.db')[source]

Bases: object

Main interface to the A‑LEMS SQLite database.

This class handles all interactions with the persistent storage: - Creating tables and indexes. - Inserting experiments, runs, events, samples. - Computing tax summaries and run state hashes. - Providing query methods for GUI and ML.

All insert operations are transactional to ensure consistency. Units are strictly enforced: times in ns, energies in µJ.

close()[source]

Close the database connection.

get_ml_data(workflow: str | None = None) → List[Dict[str, Any]][source]

Retrieve flattened ML data from the ml_features view.

This is the primary method for loading data into ML pipelines. The view already contains all features and targets in a single row per run.

Parameters:

workflow – If provided, filter by workflow_type (‘linear’ or ‘agentic’).

Returns:

List of dictionaries, each representing one run with all ML columns.

get_run(run_id: int) → Dict[str, Any] | None[source]

Retrieve a single run by its primary key.

Parameters:

run_id – Database run_id.

Returns:

Dictionary with all run columns, or None if not found.

get_runs_by_experiment(exp_id: int) → List[Dict[str, Any]][source]

Retrieve all runs belonging to an experiment, ordered by run_number.

Parameters:

exp_id – Experiment ID.

Returns:

List of run dictionaries.

get_tax_summaries(exp_id: int | None = None) → List[Dict[str, Any]][source]

Retrieve tax summary entries, optionally filtered by experiment.

Parameters:

exp_id – If provided, only summaries for runs in that experiment.

Returns:

List of tax summary dictionaries.

save_experiment(experiment_meta: Dict[str, Any], runs: List[Dict[str, Any]], hardware_info: Dict[str, Any] | None = None) → int[source]

Save a complete experiment with all its runs.

This is the main entry point for storing results from the harness. It performs the following steps: 1. Insert the experiment metadata. 2. If hardware_info provided, insert/get hardware_id. 3. Insert each run (with all metrics, events, samples). 4. After all runs are inserted, compute tax summaries by pairing

linear and agentic runs with the same run_number.

All inserts happen inside a transaction to ensure atomicity.

Parameters:

  • experiment_meta – Dictionary with keys: - name (str): experiment name - description (str): optional description - workflow_type (str): ‘linear’ or ‘agentic’ - model_name (str) - provider (str) - task_name (str) - country_code (str, default ‘US’)

  • runs – List of run result dictionaries from the harness. Each run dict must contain: - ‘ml_features’ (dict with all the 77 fields) - optionally ‘layer3_derived’ - optionally ‘execution’ - optionally ‘sustainability’ - optionally ‘orchestration_events’ - optionally ‘energy_samples’, ‘cpu_samples’, ‘interrupt_samples’

  • hardware_info – Optional dict with hardware details.

Returns:

exp_id of the newly inserted experiment.
